_comment: Canonical pyright config for Django + Pydantic. Default mode is 'basic'. Files in 'strict' get strict-mode treatment. Policy: every NEW file is added to 'strict' (or carries # pyright: strict at the top). When you touch an existing file, ratchet it up. Note: pyright reads JSON, not JSONC. The `_comment_*` keys are tolerated (unknown keys are ignored) but if your tooling rejects them, drop them.
_comment_strict: List individual files (or directory globs) that should be type-checked at strict level. Add files here as the codebase ratchets up.
_comment_django_suppressions: These rules are suppressed because django-stubs / drf-stubs do not fully model Django's dynamic metaclass machinery. Real bugs are still surfaced via reportArgumentType, reportReturnType, and reportAssignmentType (kept as warnings).
_comment_install: Install via `uv add --group dev pyright` and run `uv run pyright` (or via the Pylance extension in VS Code).
_comment_strict_on_new: Recommended editor / pre-commit setup: add # pyright: strict at the top of every NEW file. The first commit on an existing file should also add it to the strict array above. This ratchets discipline upward without requiring a one-shot conversion of the whole codebase.
